schools.summary: The priests charged to bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: 'and the LORD said unto Aaron, Thou and thy sons and thy father's house with thee shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: and thou and thy sons with thee shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood' — in answer to the people's terror, God charged Aaron and his house with the responsibility of the sanctuary and the priesthood (the bear the iniquity of the sanctuary, the priests answerable for the holy things, that no defilement or trespass go unanswered, the bear the iniquity of your priesthood, the priests answerable for their office, the figure, the priesthood appointed to bear the responsibility, to answer for the sanctuary that the people not die, the answer to the people's despair, the priests set as the appointed guard and mediators, the truth that God provides the mediator who bears the iniquity, that the people may draw near, the figure of Christ the High Priest who bears the iniquity of the holy things and of his people, Isaiah 53:11; Hebrews 9:28, that they perish not); the priests answerable for the sanctuary (the responsibility, the answer to the people's fear) — the charge (bearing the iniquity); 'bear the iniquity of your priesthood' (the priests answerable for their office, the responsibility of the priesthood, Hebrews 9:28, the figure, Christ bearing the iniquity, Isaiah 53:11, the providence, the charge, the Levites joined to follow, vv.2-4) — Calvin, Keil, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites joined to the priests to minister: 'and thy brethren also of the tribe of Levi, the tribe of thy father, bring thou with thee, that they may be joined unto thee, and minister unto thee: but thou and thy sons with thee shall minister before the tabernacle of witness' — the Levites given to be joined to the priests, to minister to them, while the priests alone served before the tabernacle (the thy brethren... of the tribe of Levi... that they may be joined unto thee, the Levites joined to the priests, the and minister unto thee, the Levites serving the priests, the thou and thy sons... shall minister before the tabernacle, the priests alone in the holy service, the figure, the orders distinguished, the Levites joined to serve, the priests in the nearer ministry, the name Levi, 'joined,' fulfilled in their being joined to the priests, the figure of the body where each has his place and order, some to the nearer service, some to the helping, the truth that God orders the ministry, each in his rank, the Levites the helpers of the priests); the Levites joined to the priests to minister (the helpers) — the joining (the Levites joined); 'thou and thy sons... shall minister before the tabernacle' (the priests alone in the holy service, the nearer ministry, the figure, the orders distinguished, each in his rank, the providence, the Levites joined, the charge of the holy things to follow, vv.3-5) — Calvin, Keil, Gill.

schools.summary: The priest's office given as a service of gift: 'therefore thou and thy sons with thee shall keep your priest's office for every thing of the altar, and within the vail; and ye shall serve: I have given your priest's office unto you as a service of gift: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death' — the gift of the office — 'I have given your priest's office unto you as a service of gift': the priesthood, with all its service of the altar and within the vail, given to Aaron and his sons as a gift of God, not seized nor earned (the witnesses noting the significance — the priesthood a free gift of God, not taken by man, but bestowed by God's choice, the very office the rebels had grasped at now confirmed as God's gift to Aaron alone, the lesson — that the priesthood is God's gift, 'no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as was Aaron,' Hebrews 5:4, the office not usurped but given, the warning of the stranger put to death the seal of its exclusiveness, the service within the vail, the nearest access, reserved to the given priesthood, the figure — the priesthood of Christ, not taken but given, 'called of God an high priest,' Hebrews 5:5-10, the figure of the office that is wholly of grace, the gift of God, the service that is honour because God bestows it, the warning that the stranger who intrudes is put to death, the office not to be usurped, as Korah and Uzziah found, 2 Chronicles 26:18, the truth that all true ministry is a gift of God, received not seized, the figure of the priesthood as God's gracious bestowal, fulfilled in Christ the called and given High Priest, the figure of the priesthood given of God, not taken by man); the priests keeping the office of the altar and within the vail (the nearest service, reserved to them) — the office (the priests' service); 'I have given your priest's office unto you as a service of gift: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death' (the priesthood given as a gift, the stranger barred on pain of death, the office God's gift, not usurped, Hebrews 5:4, the figure, Christ called of God, Hebrews 5:5, the providence, the office given, the priestly portions to follow, vv.8-19) — Calvin, Ellicott, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the priest's office as 'a service of gift' establish?

sides.position: The priesthood as God's free gift, not taken by man but bestowed by his choice - the stranger barred on pain of death the seal of its exclusiveness (2 Chronicles 26:18), a figure of the priesthood of Christ, not seized but called of God as was Aaron (Hebrews 5:4-10)

schools.summary: The priestly portion confirmed by a covenant of salt: 'all the heave offerings of the holy things, which the children of Israel offer unto the LORD, have I given thee, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: it is a covenant of salt for ever before the LORD unto thee and to thy seed with thee' — the priestly portions given by a lasting statute, a 'covenant of salt,' a perpetual and inviolable covenant (the all the heave offerings of the holy things... have I given thee, the priestly portion confirmed, the it is a covenant of salt for ever, the perpetual, unbreakable covenant, the figure, the covenant of salt, salt the token of perpetuity and incorruption, the covenant that cannot be broken, the truth that God's grant to the priesthood is sure and lasting, sealed as with salt, the figure of the enduring covenant, 'the LORD God of Israel gave the kingdom over Israel to David for ever, even to him and to his sons by a covenant of salt,' 2 Chronicles 13:5, the perpetual covenant, the incorruptible grant, a figure of the everlasting covenant of God); the priestly portion confirmed by a lasting statute (the grant) — the grant (the portion confirmed); 'it is a covenant of salt for ever' (the perpetual, inviolable covenant, salt the token of perpetuity, 2 Chronicles 13:5, the figure, the enduring covenant, the incorruptible grant, the providence, the covenant confirmed, the LORD the priests' inheritance to follow, vv.20) — Clarke, Gill.

themes: All the heave offerings of the holy things... have I given thee, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: it is a covenant of salt for ever before the LORD unto thee and to thy seed with thee

schools.summary: The LORD himself the priests' part and inheritance: 'and the LORD spake unto Aaron, Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them: I am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel' — the LORD as portion — 'thou shalt have no inheritance in their land... I am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel': the priests received no allotment of land like the other tribes, for the LORD himself was their portion and inheritance (the witnesses noting the significance — the LORD himself the inheritance of his ministers, the priests given no earthly territory, that God himself might be their portion, the lesson — the highest privilege, to have God himself as one's part, the want of an earthly inheritance more than supplied by the having of God, 'the LORD is the portion of mine inheritance... I have a goodly heritage,' Psalm 16:5-6, 'the LORD is my portion, saith my soul,' Lamentations 3:24, 'God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever,' Psalm 73:26, the figure — the believer whose portion is God, not the things of earth, the saints whose inheritance is the LORD himself, the truth that those who serve God most nearly are given God himself as their reward, 'the LORD is their inheritance,' Deuteronomy 10:9; 18:2, the figure of the heavenly-minded who have God for their portion and seek no earthly possession, fulfilled in the saints whose inheritance is God in Christ, 'heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ,' Romans 8:17, the figure of God himself the all-sufficient portion of his people, the figure of the LORD the inheritance of those who serve him); the priests given no earthly inheritance (no allotment of land, Deuteronomy 10:9) — the want (no earthly portion); 'I am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel' (the LORD himself the priests' inheritance, God their portion, Psalm 16:5; 73:26, the figure, the saints whose inheritance is God, Romans 8:17, the providence, the LORD their portion, the tithe to the Levites to follow, vv.21-24) — Calvin, Keil, Henry, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does 'I am thy part and thine inheritance' establish?

sides.position: The priests given no earthly land, that God himself might be their portion - the highest privilege of having God for one's part (Psalm 16:5-6; 73:26; Lamentations 3:24), a figure of the believer whose inheritance is the LORD himself, the heirs of God and joint-heirs with Christ (Deuteronomy 10:9; Romans 8:17)

themes: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them: I am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel

a figure of the believer whose inheritance is the LORD himself, the heirs of God and joint-heirs with Christ (Deuteronomy 10:9; Romans 8:17)

schools.summary: The tithe given to the Levites for their service: 'and, behold, I have given the children of Levi all the tenth in Israel for an inheritance, for their service which they serve, even the service of the tabernacle of the congregation' — the grant — 'I have given the children of Levi all the tenth in Israel for an inheritance, for their service': in place of an earthly inheritance, God gave the Levites all the tithe of Israel, in return for their service of the tabernacle (the witnesses noting the significance — the tithe the Levites' inheritance and wage, the tenth of all Israel's increase given them, since they had no land, as the reward of their service, the lesson — God's provision for those who serve him, that they who give themselves to the ministry be sustained by the people, the Levites freed from the labor of the land, provided by the tithe, that they might serve, 'the labourer is worthy of his hire,' Luke 10:7, 'the Lord hath ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel,' 1 Corinthians 9:14, the tithe a recognition of God's ownership and a provision for his ministers, the figure — the support of the ministry, the people sustaining those who serve at the altar, that the work of God's house be maintained, the truth that God provides for his servants through the offerings of his people, the tithe their inheritance 'for their service,' the figure of the honour and maintenance due to those who labour in the things of God, the ministry sustained by the people of God, the figure of God's provision for his ministers through the tithe of his people); the tithe given the Levites in place of land (their inheritance, since they had no allotment) — the tithe (the Levites' inheritance); 'for their service which they serve, even the service of the tabernacle of the congregation' (the tithe the reward of the Levites' service, the provision for the ministry, Luke 10:7; 1 Corinthians 9:14, the figure, the support of the ministry, the providence, the tithe given, the Levites' service the ground, the tithe of the tithe to follow, vv.25-28) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Clarke, Gill.

schools.summary: The people barred from the tabernacle lest they die: 'neither must the children of Israel henceforth come nigh the tabernacle of the congregation, lest they bear sin, and die' — the common people barred from the tabernacle, lest they trespass and die, the Levites standing between (the the children of Israel henceforth come nigh the tabernacle, the people forbidden to draw near, the lest they bear sin, and die, the danger of trespass, the death, the figure, the people kept from the holy, the Levites the appointed guard between them and the sanctuary, the answer to the people's terror, the bounds set, the mediators standing, the truth that the holy God is approached only through the appointed mediators, the people barred lest they die, the figure of the way of access reserved, the need of the mediator, fulfilled in Christ 'the new and living way,' Hebrews 10:20, through whom alone the people draw near); the people barred from the tabernacle (the bounds) — the bar (the people forbidden); 'lest they bear sin, and die' (the danger of trespass and death, the people kept from the holy, Hebrews 10:20, the figure, the need of the mediator, the providence, the people barred, the Levites bearing the iniquity to follow, vv.23) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites doing the service and bearing the iniquity, having no inheritance: 'but the Levites shall do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they shall bear their iniquity: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations, that among the children of Israel they have no inheritance' — the Levites were to do the tabernacle service and bear the responsibility, having no land inheritance among Israel (the the Levites shall do the service of the tabernacle, the Levites' work, the they shall bear their iniquity, answerable for any trespass in the service, the among the children of Israel they have no inheritance, no land, the LORD their portion, vv.20, the figure, the Levites set to the service and the responsibility, having no earthly inheritance, the truth that those who serve God are answerable for the service, and provided not with land but with God and his tithe, the figure of the ministers who bear the charge of God's house, their inheritance not of earth, the Levites the appointed servants, the responsibility theirs, the provision the tithe); the Levites doing the service and bearing the iniquity (the responsibility) — the charge (the service and responsibility); 'among the children of Israel they have no inheritance' (the Levites without land, the LORD their portion, vv.20, the figure, the ministers whose inheritance is not of earth, the providence, the Levites' service, the tithe their portion to follow, vv.24) — Wesley, Gill.

schools.summary: The tithe given the Levites as their inheritance instead of land: 'but the tithes of the children of Israel, which they offer as an heave offering unto the LORD, I have given to the Levites to inherit: therefore I have said unto them, Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance' — the tithe, offered to the LORD, given to the Levites as their inheritance, in place of land (the the tithes... I have given to the Levites to inherit, the tithe the Levites' inheritance, the therefore... Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance, no land, the tithe instead, the figure, the tithe given the Levites as their inheritance, the offering of the people their portion, the truth that the Levites' inheritance was the tithe, not land, God providing through the people's offerings, the figure of the ministry sustained by the people's gifts, the tithe the appointed portion, the provision of God for his servants, the tithe their heritage, the heave offering offered to God then given to the Levites); the tithe given the Levites as their inheritance (the offering their portion) — the inheritance (the tithe); 'Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance' (no land, the tithe instead, the Levites' portion, the figure, the ministry sustained by the people's gifts, the providence, the tithe their inheritance, the tithe of the tithe to follow, vv.25-28) — JFB, Gill.

themes: But the tithes of the children of Israel, which they offer as an heave offering unto the LORD, I have given to the Levites to inherit: therefore I have said unto them, Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance

schools.summary: The LORD speaking concerning the Levites' own tithe: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — the LORD speaking to Moses, now concerning the tithe the Levites themselves must give, the tithe of the tithe (vv.26-28, the speaking, God's command, the new section, the occasion, after the tithe was given the Levites, now the law that they too must tithe, giving a tenth of what they received to the priests, the figure, the Levites, though provided by the tithe, themselves to give a tithe, the truth that even those who live by the offerings owe their portion to God, the figure of all owing to God, the ministers themselves giving of what they receive, God speaking concerning the Levites' tithe, showing that all, even the servants of the sanctuary, owe their tribute to God); the LORD speaking to Moses (the tithe of the tithe) — the new section (the Levites' tithe); the LORD spake unto Moses — the Levites' tithe introduced (the providence, God ordering that the Levites too tithe) — Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites' heave offering, a tenth of the tithe: 'thus speak unto the Levites, and say unto them, When ye take of the children of Israel the tithes which I have given you from them for your inheritance, then ye shall offer up an heave offering of it for the LORD, even a tenth part of the tithe' — the Levites, receiving the tithe, to offer a tenth of it as a heave offering to the LORD, for the priests (the when ye take... the tithes which I have given you... for your inheritance, the Levites' tithe received, the then ye shall offer up an heave offering of it for the LORD, the Levites' offering, the even a tenth part of the tithe, the tithe of the tithe, the figure, the Levites themselves tithing, giving a tenth of what they received to the priests, the truth that the ministers, though provided, themselves give to God, the figure of all owing tribute to God, even those who live by the offerings, the order that the Levites' tithe went to the priests, the firstfruits of their inheritance); the Levites receiving the tithe (their inheritance) — the tithe (received); 'ye shall offer up an heave offering... even a tenth part of the tithe' (the Levites' tithe to the priests, the tithe of the tithe, the figure, the ministers themselves giving to God, the providence, the Levites' tithe, the offering reckoned to follow, vv.27-28) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites' offering reckoned as the corn and wine: 'and this your heave offering shall be reckoned unto you, as though it were the corn of the threshingfloor, and as the fulness of the winepress' — the Levites' tithe of the tithe reckoned to them as if it were their own harvest of corn and wine (the this your heave offering shall be reckoned unto you, the Levites' offering counted, the as though it were the corn of the threshingfloor, and as the fulness of the winepress, reckoned as their own produce, their harvest, the figure, the Levites' offering counted as their own increase, that they too give of their firstfruits, the truth that the tithe they received was reckoned their harvest, of which they offered to God, the figure of the ministers' portion counted as their increase, the offering of the firstfruits of what they received, the order that the Levites, having no field, yet had their tithe reckoned as a harvest from which to give to God); the Levites' offering counted to them (reckoned) — the reckoning (the offering counted); 'as though it were the corn of the threshingfloor, and as the fulness of the winepress' (reckoned as their own produce, their harvest, the figure, the ministers' portion counted as their increase, the providence, the offering reckoned, the heave offering of the best to follow, vv.28-29) — Wesley, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites giving the LORD's heave offering to the priest: 'thus ye also shall offer an heave offering unto the LORD of all your tithes, which ye receive of the children of Israel; and ye shall give thereof the LORD's heave offering to Aaron the priest' — the Levites to offer a heave offering of all their tithes, giving it to Aaron the priest (the ye also shall offer an heave offering unto the LORD of all your tithes, the Levites' offering of their tithes, the ye shall give thereof the LORD's heave offering to Aaron the priest, the offering given to the priest, the figure, the Levites' offering to the LORD given to the priest, the order of the giving, the truth that the Levites' tribute to God went to the priests, the higher order, the figure of the orders of the ministry, the Levites giving to the priests, the priests the higher, the offering of the Levites passing to Aaron, the appointed channel); the Levites offering of their tithes (the heave offering) — the offering (the Levites' tithe); 'ye shall give thereof the LORD's heave offering to Aaron the priest' (the offering given to Aaron, the priest the receiver, the figure, the Levites giving to the higher order, the providence, the offering to the priest, the best to be offered to follow, vv.29-30) — Clarke, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites offering the best, the hallowed part: 'out of all your gifts ye shall offer every heave offering of the LORD, of all the best thereof, even the hallowed part thereof out of it' — the Levites to offer the best of all they received, the hallowed part, as the heave offering (the out of all your gifts ye shall offer every heave offering of the LORD, the offering of their gifts, the of all the best thereof, even the hallowed part, the best, the choicest given, the figure, the best offered to God, the choicest of what the Levites received, the truth that God claims the best, the hallowed part, even from his ministers, the figure of the firstfruits and the best devoted to God, the choicest of the gift, the order that the Levites offer not the worst but the best, the hallowed part, as God requires the best of all); the Levites offering of all their gifts (the heave offering) — the offering (of their gifts); 'of all the best thereof, even the hallowed part' (the best and hallowed part offered, the choicest given, the figure, God claiming the best, the providence, the best offered, the warning to offer the best to follow, vv.30-32) — Wesley, Gill.

schools.summary: The rest counted to the Levites as their increase: 'therefore thou shalt say unto them, When ye have heaved the best thereof from it, then it shall be counted unto the Levites as the increase of the threshingfloor, and as the increase of the winepress' — when the Levites had offered the best (the tithe of the tithe), the rest was theirs to use freely, counted as their own increase (the when ye have heaved the best thereof from it, the best offered to God, the then it shall be counted unto the Levites as the increase of the threshingfloor, and as the increase of the winepress, the rest reckoned as their own harvest, theirs to eat, the figure, the rest, after the offering, the Levites' own to enjoy, the truth that, the tribute paid, the rest is the minister's own, freely to be used, the figure of the portion that, after God's part is given, is the servant's own, the liberty of the Levites to use the remainder, the offering made, the rest their own increase); the best offered first (the heave offering) — the offering (the best heaved); 'it shall be counted unto the Levites as the increase of the threshingfloor' (the rest reckoned as their own harvest, theirs to use, the figure, the portion the minister's own after God's part, the providence, the rest theirs, the warning to offer the best to follow, vv.31-32) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Therefore thou shalt say unto them, When ye have heaved the best thereof from it, then it shall be counted unto the Levites as the increase of the threshingfloor, and as the increase of the winepress

schools.summary: The Levites eating the rest as the reward of their service: 'and ye shall eat it in every place, ye and your households: for it is your reward for your service in the tabernacle of the congregation' — the Levites, having offered the best, might eat the rest anywhere, with their households, as the reward of their service (the ye shall eat it in every place, ye and your households, the rest eaten freely, not bound to the holy place, since it was now common, the for it is your reward for your service, the wage of their ministry, the figure, the rest the Levites' wage, eaten freely as their own, the truth that the minister's portion is his reward, freely his after the offering, the figure of the labourer worthy of his hire, the reward of the service of God, the provision of God for his servants, the rest their reward, 'the labourer is worthy of his reward,' 1 Timothy 5:18, the liberty and reward of those who serve in the house of God); the Levites eating freely with their households (the rest common) — the liberty (eaten anywhere); 'it is your reward for your service' (the wage of the Levites' ministry, the reward, 1 Timothy 5:18, the figure, the labourer worthy of his hire, the providence, the reward, the warning to offer the best to follow, vv.32) — Calvin, Gill.

schools.summary: The warning to offer the best lest they pollute the holy things and die: 'and ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best of it: neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, lest ye die' — if the Levites first offered the best, they bore no sin in eating the rest; but they must not pollute the holy things, lest they die (the ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best, no sin if the best be first offered, the neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, the warning against profaning the holy, the lest ye die, the danger of death, the figure, the warning to give God his best first, that the rest be enjoyed without sin, the truth that to keep back the best, or to profane the holy things, brings death, the figure of the reverence due the holy things, the best given to God, the holy not polluted, the closing warning of the chapter, the holy things to be honored, the best offered, lest death follow, the reverence that honors God with the first and best, and dares not profane his holy things); no sin if the best be first offered (the rest then free) — the warning (no sin when the best is given); 'neither shall ye pollute the holy things... lest ye die' (the warning against profaning the holy, the danger of death, the figure, the reverence due the holy things, the providence, the warning, closing the chapter) — Wesley, Gill.
